title: Call with Michael
summary: Vinnie Micciche, Michael Donovan, and Jason Branham discussed marketing hooks, content flow, and shifting technology terminology to "automotive co-pilot." Vinnie Micciche shared promising Sophia data, emphasizing clear, concise messaging highlighting unique value propositions and driving revenue by targeting larger dealer groups. Vinnie Micciche's messaging vision for speed, efficiency, and advanced technology was adopted verbatim for marketing content.

decisions.description: Adopt 'automotive co-pilot' terminology for technology features.
Vinnie Micciche's vision for marketing messaging to be adopted verbatim.
Shift business focus from 'mom and pop' to larger dealer groups and OEMs.
Reject posts using older terminology like 'busy BDC' for marketing content.

actionItems.task: Create posts using Vinnie Micciche's clear and simple messaging.
Share the link to the Perplexity tool with Vinnie Micciche.
Compile and post verbatim hooks with short captions for a month.
Integrate new messaging into new website content development.

tensions: Vinnie Micciche's frustration with Michael Donovan's post content complexity.
Mitch from Vconic's hesitation about using 'Co-pilot' due to Microsoft's push.
Vinnie Micciche's concern about existing website content relevance.
Vinnie Micciche's past frustration with Group One not developing equity mining tools.
referencesToPast: Vinnie Micciche's prior frustration with a colleague's content choice.
Vinnie Micciche recalling owning 'Automotive Copilot' term.
Mention of Matt previously lacking time for data analysis.
Vinnie Micciche's historical example of Group One not developing equity mining tools.
Vinnie Micciche's concern about existing content pages being copies of old site.

contributions.description: Initiated discussion on marketing hooks and their review.
Explained using Chat GPT for content focusing on people, tech, and dealer needs.
Emphasized collaborative discussion for content flow over next two months.
Suggested 'our technology makes them faster' over 'dialer' for clarity.
Agreed with 'co-pilots' for technology, noting it makes them faster.
Recalled owning 'Automotive Copilot' term, clarifying its use for human-AI coaching.
Stressed messaging appealing to diverse dealer personas, from 'old school' to AI-savvy.
Preferred thought leadership: 'humans working with AI instead of AI replacing humans'.
Shared Sophia's success data, suggesting it could be a scalable, standalone product.
Emphasized concise messaging directly on graphics for quick attention.
Highlighted unified communications (calls, texts, emails in one place) as key offering.
Provided powerful summary: 'We call faster, respond faster, capture everything, connect channels'.
Provided update on Link agreement, detailing revised pricing structure.
Demonstrated a Perplexity project for generating LinkedIn posts.
Proposed shifting business focus to larger dealer groups and OEMs.
